# dokploy_domain (Resource)

A domain routing external traffic to a Dokploy application.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dokploy_domain" "web" {
  application_id   = dokploy_application.api.id
  host             = "api.example.com"
  port             = 8080
  https            = true
  certificate_type = "letsencrypt"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `application_id` (String) Application the domain routes to. Changing this forces replacement.
- `host` (String) Domain hostname, e.g. `api.example.com`.
- `port` (Number) Container port to route traffic to. Required by the Dokploy API.

### Optional

- `certificate_type` (String) Certificate type: `none` or `letsencrypt`.
- `https` (Boolean) Whether to serve over HTTPS.
- `path` (String) Path prefix to route. Defaults to `/`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import dokploy_domain.web <domainId>
```
